page: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and bzip3 single-threaded decompression scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is average, as shown by stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores falling in the middle 50%. However, memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with both small-block and large-block read-only memory bandwidth testing in the bottom 25%. Similarly, database operations and software compilation benchmarks show weaker performance, placing in the bottom 25%.

Given its hardware profile, the D4as_v6 is suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, text processing, and lightweight Redis operations, which align with its average-to-strong benchmark results in these areas. It is less suitable for memory-bandwidth-intensive applications, large-scale database operations, or complex multi-threaded tasks like software compilation. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a dedicated CPU allocation and modern AMD EPYC architecture at a entry-level resource density, making it a viable option for workloads requiring consistent single-core performance without heavy memory throughput demands.
description: The Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, though it supports Premium Storage. Architecturally, it supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. However, memory read bandwidth and database operations are weaker, landing in the bottom 25%. Multi-core performance and web serving throughput remain average. This instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and text processing tasks that benefit from dedicated single-core execution but do not require high memory bandwidth or local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance, suitable for web serving and text processing.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is an AMD EPYC-powered general-purpose VM with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, offering strong single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC virtual machine delivering strong single-core performance and balanced memory for general-purpose computing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Features 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Delivers strong single-core performance in the top 25% of benchmarks
Lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage integration
Suited for static web serving, text processing, and light Redis operations
Tradeoffs include lower memory read bandwidth and database performance
